elements.sent-2411-001: We study linearly independent complex line fields on almost-complex manifolds, which is a topic of long-standing interest in differential topology and complex geometry.
elements.sent-2411-002: A necessary condition for the existence of such fields is the vanishing of appropriate virtual Chern classes.
elements.sent-2411-003: We prove that this condition is also sufficient for the existence of one, two, or three linearly independent complex line fields over certain manifolds.
elements.sent-2411-004: More generally, our results hold for a wider class of complex bundles over CW complexes.
elements.sent-2411-006: Complex line fields, complex vector fields, complex span, complex projective span, almost-complex manifolds, Postnikov systems, \(k\)-invariants, spectral sequence, Chern classes.. \setcounter{tocdepth {1.}}
elements.sent-2411-007: Characteristic classes are classically studied cohomological invariants of vector bundles that sometimes obstruct the existence of linearly independent sections.
elements.sent-2411-008: For example, given a complex $m$-plane vector bundle $\xi \colon~ \C^m \to E \to X$ over a CW complex \(X\), the $i$-th Chern class $c_i(\xi) \in \co^{2i (B;\Z)$ is the primary obstruction to the existence of $m\!+\!1\!-\!i$ linearly independent sections of $\xi$.}
elements.sent-2411-009: If $\varepsilon^n \colon~ \C^n \to \C^n \times X \to X$ denotes the trivial complex \(n\)-plane bundle, then a nonvanishing section of $\xi$ is equivalent to a rank \(1\) trivial subbundle \(\varepsilon^1 \subseteq \xi\).
elements.sent-2411-010: More generally, the existence of $r$ linearly independent sections of $\xi$ is equivalent to the existence of an embedding of $\varepsilon^r$ into $\xi$.
elements.sent-2411-011: This leads to a numerical invariant of \(\xi\) called the (complex) span, defined as \begin{equation}\cspan(\xi) \coloneqq \max\{r \in \Z_{\geq 0} \mid \varepsilon^{r} \subseteq \xi\}.\end{equation}.
